Svatl.
[Pahlavas arc identified with the Parthians, who are known to
have occupied parts of north-western India in a few centuries before
and after Christ. Rudradaman's inscription mentions a Pahlava
named Suvi^akha, as the governor of Anarta and Sura§tra.
Ksmboja is mentioned by Kalidasa (Raghu IV) and in the
Mahabharata (VII 4.6). Hence it is located by scholars to the
south of Kashmir; and its western boundaries might have extended
to Kafiristan. Yaska refers (1.2.1.4) to the particular usage of the
root &av in the sense of fto go* among the Kambojas. It is learned
that this peculiarity is still current in the Galcha-speaking areas of
Pamir and Badakhshan. It is possible that during the period of
Varahamihira there might have been a settlement of the Kambojas
in the south-western region of India.

Division of Globe XIV 176
Sindhu-Sauvira is identified with Sindh-Sagar Doab, the region
between the Jhelumand the Indus. In the Junagadh inscription also
